items.prompt: Strict Swiss-grid minimalism. Composition is built on a visible or implied modular grid — every element snaps to grid lines, deliberate column-and-row alignment carries the whole page. Forms are reduced to essential geometric primitives — clean rectangles, perfect circles, single-weight lines. Typographic character is sans-serif, set with rigorous spacing. Color behavior: warm ivory background #FAF6F0 dominates as the main field (about 80% area); primary dark navy #1D2A44 forms a confident vertical block on the left (about 15% area); accent warm caramel #C8966E appears only as one thin horizontal line near the lower right corner (under 5% of canvas). No gradients, no shadows, no texture, no decoration. Negative space carries as much weight as filled areas. Composed as a 1280x720 full-bleed PPT background. The center 60% of the canvas is deliberately calm and empty, designed to receive a slide title overlaid in SVG. NO text of any kind anywhere in the image. Color values are rendering guidance only — do not display HEX codes or color names as text.
items.alt_text: Minimalist Swiss grid cover background in warm ivory, dark navy and caramel

items.prompt: Strict Swiss-grid minimalism. Composition is built on a visible or implied modular grid. Forms are reduced to essential geometric primitives — clean rectangles, perfect circles, single-weight lines. Color behavior: warm ivory background #FAF6F0 (about 70%); all linework and geometric figures in primary dark navy #1D2A44 (about 25%). Accent warm caramel #C8966E appears on a single highlighted circle only (about 5%). No gradients, no shadows, no texture, no decoration. Composed as a 1280x720 image. The visual composition is a abstract symbolic illustration merging Eastern Zen rock garden concentric circles with Western Bauhaus geometric architectural lines, aligned to a rigorous grid. NO text, letters, numbers, signs, watermarks, or written symbols. Color values are rendering guidance only.
items.alt_text: Abstract line art merging Japanese Zen garden with Bauhaus geometry
